name: The Andes Hotel
description: Casual dining, cocktails, and occasional live music right in the village.
seasonal: Hours may vary seasonally — call ahead in winter.

name: Brushland Eating House
description: Excellent, straightforward farm-to-table dining in a converted farmhouse. Menus change often based on local sourcing.
seasonal: Schedule changes seasonally — always check website before visiting.

name: Peekamoose Restaurant
description: High-end seasonal dining further out in Big Indian. Solid wine list and menu.
seasonal: Closed Tuesdays & Wednesdays. Reservations strongly recommended.

name: Pakatakan Farmers Market
description: Large Saturday-morning market running May through Oct/Nov. Hub for regional meats, cheeses, produce, and crafts.

name: Belleayre Mountain
description: State-run ski mountain featuring a gondola and 50+ trails. Reliable grooming and open late November through March.
seasonal: Night skiing on select dates; check website for conditions.

name: Hunter Mountain
description: One of the largest ski resorts in the Catskills with 67 trails and extensive snowmaking. About 30 miles east of Andes. Strong intermediate and expert terrain.
seasonal: 100% snowmaking coverage keeps the mountain open even in warm winters.

name: Windham Mountain
description: Family-friendly resort with 54 trails and a relaxed Catskills vibe. About 35 miles east. Known for excellent grooming and a welcoming atmosphere for beginners.
seasonal: First Tracks program offers early morning groomed runs before lifts open to the public.

name: Plattekill Mountain
description: Independently owned ski mountain with an authentic, uncrowded vibe. Typically operates Fridays through Sundays starting early December.

name: Balsam Lake Mtn. Trail
description: Moderate 5.5-mile hike to a restored fire tower. Features great views, but winter/spring conditions require microspikes.
seasonal: Trail may be icy or snowy December-March. Microspikes recommended.

name: Catskill Center Trails
description: Well-maintained preserve trails open dawn to dusk. Good spot to pull official maps and regional hiking info.

name: Delhi
description: The main county hub for practical logistics. Head here for the full-size Price Chopper, Walgreens, ACE Hardware, and Tractor Supply.

name: Margaretville
description: Nearby town useful for picking up supplies, a dedicated liquor store, and basic dining options.

name: Andes
description: Walkable strip of independent shops, dynamic restaurants, and local mainstays.

name: Andes Society of History and Culture
Andes General Store & Deli
description: A fresh addition to the village for general groceries, daily staples, and convenience items.  Decent sandwiches.

name: Cafe Mutsi
description: Good spot for daytime coffee and light snacks. Transitions to a focused dinner service on Friday nights.

name: Wayside Cider
description: Local cidery with a spacious taproom and outdoor space. Good for a casual drink or a light bite on weekends.  Upstairs of restaurant/bar has an expansive game room with pool, darts.

name: Andes Dragonfly Diner
description: Classic hometown diner for solid breakfast and lunch. Watch for shifting seasonal hours.  Take out available.

name: Dana's Place
description: No-nonsense, reliable local bar open late on weekends and most weeknights.  Typical pub grub menu.

name: Estro
description: Curated mid-20th-century European furniture, lighting fixtures, and vintage artwork.  Also has sandwiches, coffee and small selection of pantry items.

name: Diamond Hollow Books
description: Independent bookshop that features highly specialized collections alongside regular local literary and community events.

name: Rae's
description: A solid option for dinner and drinks, serving modern takes on classic comfort food, cocktails, and natural wine.

name: Tomato Tomahto
description: Casual Italian Main Street spot specializing in fresh, reliable lunch options. Good stop to grab a bite while running errands in town.

name: EIGHTYMain
description: High-end, Italian and Argentinian-influenced restaurant focusing heavily on regional Catskill ingredients. Bar and counter seating are kept open for walk-ins.

name: Hollow
description: Storefront cafe with attached restaurant serving a tight seasonal dinner menu from Wednesday through Saturday. They do not take reservations, so expect to wait during peak weekend hours.

name: Ice Cream Shop
description: Classic, seasonal walk-up window serving standard soft serve, hard ice cream, and classic sundaes right in the center of the village.

name: Blue Bee Cafe
description: Serving excellent homemade breads, sandwiches, quiches, and baked goods in a casual, cafe-bistro atmosphere.

name: Baker's Grimm
description: Small-batch, family-owned village bakery focused on traditional organic sourdough breads, fresh bagels, pastries, and simple daytime lunch specials like daily soups and sandwiches.

name: This & That
description: Highly curated antique, vintage, and home decor shop. Features a rotating mix of housewares, furniture, and unique objects.

name: The Stonehouse
description: Tiny, packed boutique operating out of a former barber shop. Specializes in vintage Moroccan Berber rugs, global textiles, artisan homewares, and high-quality incense.

name: The Lost Bookshop
description: Independent community bookstore. Well-curated selection spanning contemporary fiction, classics, sci-fi, and regional Catskill history, alongside regular community book discussions.

name: Luck Dragon
description: Creative workspace and supply shop carrying high-quality art supplies, fabrics, notions, and books. They host niche weekend workshops ranging from sewing to electronics soldering.

name: Double Z Farm
description: Local family farm and a staple vendor at the Delhi Farmers' Market. Known for high-quality pasture-raised meats including lamb, beef, chicken, and rabbit, alongside unique alpaca wool products.

name: Kitchen Cheetah
description: Kitchen and home accessories store carrying everything from high-end cookware to cookbooks, specialty pantry imports, and goods from local farmers.

name: Freshtown
description: Full-service supermarket.  A standard, reliable stop for high-volume grocery runs, featuring a full deli, bakery, fresh produce, and daily essentials.

name: Good Cheap Food
description: Best local spot for bulk organic dry goods, regional cheeses, fresh produce, and specialty health food staples.  Extensive bulk spice and tea selection.  Not necessarily cheap.. But it is good.

name: Price Chopper
description: The most reliable, high-volume grocery option in the immediate area for major chain staples, pharmacy needs, and standard household essentials.

name: Russell's General Store
description: Great destination for morning breakfast sandwiches and basic goods. Note: strict no-dogs policy.

name: First Bloom Corner Store
description: A well-curated daytime corner stop open Thursday through Sunday for regional goods and quick provisions.

name: Byebrook Farm
description: Local farmstand famous for stocking Gilligan's ice cream alongside basic farm staples.

name: Burn Ayr Farm
description: Seasonal daytime produce and farm market operating from May through October. Tends to close early by mid-afternoon.

name: Brookside Farm
description: Self-serve roadside stand featuring a 24-hour meat case and local maple products.

name: Hamden General Store
description: Updated historic crossroads store stocking essential groceries, pantry imports, local craft beer, cider, and fresh regional produce. A reliable, quiet spot to grab a morning coffee.

name: Tiny's
description: Daytime cafe operating right out of the back of the Hamden General serving high-quality breakfast and lunch sandwiches (Get the chopped cheese!)

name: Magpies
description: Specializes in hyper-seasonal sweet and savory pies, alongside fresh homemade biscuit breakfast sandwiches and coffee.

name: Pepacton Reservoir
description: Massive, scenic reservoir offering quiet drives and fishing. Note: requires a free NYC DEP permit and NYS fishing license before hitting the water.
seasonal: Fall foliage is spectacular mid-October. Winter views are dramatic.

name: Catskill Recreation Center
description: Area indoor recreation center consisting of 24k sqft that houses a 6-lane pool, locker rooms, a fully-equipped fitness room, and group-exercise/multi-purpose rooms. Daily rate of $18/$10 fir Adult/Child
